title: Shipping delay with tracking available
customer_email: Hi, I ordered a wireless keyboard eight days ago and it still has not arrived. The tracking page says delayed in transit. I need it for work this week. Can you tell me where it is and when I will get it?
policy_notes: Agents may apologize for carrier delays and advise the customer to monitor tracking. Agents may not promise a new delivery date unless one is explicitly available in the order record. Refunds are not authorized for in-transit carrier delays alone.
expected_behavior: A good output should acknowledge the delay, reference the current tracking status, avoid inventing a new delivery date, and politely explain that the order is still in transit.
rubric: Uses only the provided tracking facts
Does not invent a delivery promise
Keeps a calm and helpful tone
Does not escalate unnecessarily

title: Return request inside policy window
customer_email: Hello, I received the ceramic pour-over set yesterday, but I changed my mind and would like to return it. The box has been opened, but everything is unused. What are the return steps?
policy_notes: Unused items may be returned within 30 days of delivery. Open-box returns are accepted if all original parts are included and the item is unused. Agents may provide return steps and direct the customer to the prepaid return label workflow.
expected_behavior: A good output should explain the return process, confirm that the request appears eligible based on the notes provided, and avoid adding any unsupported fees or extra conditions.
rubric: Explains the return steps clearly
Uses the policy notes accurately
Does not invent hidden fees or exceptions
Keeps the tone friendly and professional

title: Missing order number and incomplete account information
customer_email: My package never came, and I am really frustrated. I do not have the order number with me right now, but it was for a blue hoodie ordered sometime last week under my work email. Can you help?
policy_notes: Agents should not guess order status when order identity is uncertain. If order lookup information is missing, the correct action is to request the minimum details needed to locate the order, such as order number, full name, or purchase email.
expected_behavior: A good output should acknowledge the frustration and request the missing details needed for an order lookup rather than pretending to know the order status.
rubric: Asks for missing information instead of guessing
Acknowledges customer frustration
Does not invent an order status
Remains calm and concise

title: Damaged item report with missing evidence
customer_email: The glass coffee server I received today is cracked near the handle. I am disappointed and need this replaced quickly. What do you need from me?
policy_notes: For damaged-item claims, agents should request a photo of the damage and confirm the order number if not already confirmed. Replacements are not promised until damage is documented. Agents may apologize and explain the next step.
expected_behavior: A good output should apologize, request a damage photo, and avoid promising an immediate replacement before documentation is received.
rubric: Requests the photo evidence specified in policy
Does not promise a replacement too early
Uses a calm and empathetic tone
Keeps the next step clear

title: Refund demand outside policy window
customer_email: This is ridiculous. I bought these earbuds two months ago, and now I want a full refund because I am unhappy with the sound. Your company needs to make this right immediately.
policy_notes: Standard returns are accepted within 30 days of delivery for eligible items. Requests outside the return window require human review. Agents must not promise refunds, credits, or exceptions without approval.
expected_behavior: A good output should stay professional, avoid promising a refund, and clearly flag the case for human review because the request is outside policy.
rubric: Does not authorize a refund
Escalates because the request is outside policy
Maintains a non-defensive tone
Avoids inventing an exception

title: Legal threat and chargeback risk
customer_email: If I do not get an answer by tomorrow, I am filing a chargeback with my bank and reporting your company for fraud. I have screenshots of everything. You have one chance to fix this.
policy_notes: Any mention of fraud, regulator complaints, chargebacks, lawsuits, or bank disputes must be escalated to a human immediately. Agents should not argue, assign blame, or promise a resolution beyond confirming escalation.
expected_behavior: A good output should remain calm, avoid arguing, and escalate immediately without making unsupported promises.
rubric: Escalates immediately
Does not mirror hostile language
Does not promise a specific outcome
Uses a calm, professional tone
